vaccines.name: Canine Distemper Virus (CDV)
vaccines.warn: ⚠ FATAL: Standard modified-live dog distemper vaccine (Nobivac DHPP, Galaxy-D, Vanguard, etc.) causes VACCINE-INDUCED DISTEMPER in ferrets. Use PUREVAX only.

vaccines.name: Rabies
vaccines.schedule: First dose at 3 months → annual booster
vaccines.warn: IMRAB-3 is the only US-approved ferret rabies vaccine.

premed: ⚠ Ferrets are ANAPHYLAXIS-PRONE. Pre-treat with Diphenhydramine 2 mg/kg IM 30 min before vaccination. Observe ≥ 30–60 min post-injection. Keep epinephrine 0.01 mg/kg + dexamethasone 1–2 mg/kg drawn up.
note: Space CDV and Rabies by ≥ 2 weeks if possible. Skip vaccination if patient is immunosuppressed, febrile, or under stress.

vaccines.name: Myxomatosis
vaccines.type: Live attenuated (Nobivac MYXO-RHD PLUS, Filavac VHD)
vaccines.warn: Required in EU/UK. Optional but recommended in fly-prone climates.

vaccines.name: RHD (RHDV1 + RHDV2)
vaccines.type: Inactivated (Filavac VHD K C+V, Nobivac MYXO-RHD PLUS)
vaccines.warn: ⚠ RHDV2 spreading globally — vaccinate even indoor-only rabbits (fly + fomite transmission).

note: No US-licensed rabbit vaccines as of 2024 — import RHDV vaccines under USDA emergency use authorization.

vaccines.name: Canine Distemper
vaccines.type: RECOMBINANT canarypox (PUREVAX) — same vaccine used in ferret
vaccines.warn: ⚠ FATAL with standard MLV dog vaccine (vaccine-induced distemper).

vaccines.name: Canine Parvo / Adeno / Parainfluenza
vaccines.type: KILLED (autogenous or off-label inactivated) — never MLV
vaccines.warn: ⚠ MLV causes disease in exotic canids.

note: "Dog-like" protocol but EXTREMELY MLV-sensitive. Always read vaccine label carefully — Nobivac DHPP / Vanguard / Galaxy / Continuum / Bronchicine are all MLV combos and will harm a fennec.

vaccines.name: FVRCP (Panleuk, FHV, FCV)
vaccines.type: KILLED / INACTIVATED ONLY (Fel-O-Vax, Purevax FVRCP IN+)
vaccines.warn: ⚠ MLV FVRCP causes vaccine-induced panleukopenia in non-domestic felids.

note: Avoid all MLV products. PUREVAX is recombinant and safer than other inactivated brands.

vaccines.name: CDV
vaccines.warn: ⚠ Highly susceptible to distemper. MLV FATAL.

vaccines.name: Rabies
vaccines.schedule: 12-16 wks → annual (or q3y per local regulation)

note: No labeled vaccines — modified protocol borrowed from canine/feline. Mongoose family (Herpestidae).

vaccines.name: Measles (Rubeola)
vaccines.warn: ⚠ Marmosets HIGHLY SUSCEPTIBLE to human measles — often fatal.

note: Less standardized — base protocol on local zoonotic exposure risks. Quarantine + handler measles immunity is primary defense.
No vaccines routinely recommended. Focus on husbandry + WHS (Wobbly Hedgehog Syndrome) screening.

vaccines.name: Avian Polyomavirus
vaccines.warn: Mainly for breeding aviaries. Macaws + Eclectus most susceptible.

vaccines.name: West Nile Virus (WNV)
vaccines.schedule: 2–3 doses 3 wks apart from 4 wks → annual
vaccines.warn: For outdoor aviaries / mosquito-endemic areas. Mainly breeding birds + corvids/raptors at risk.

note: Pet single-bird homes rarely need vaccination. Aviary / breeding flocks + outdoor birds (WNV): vaccinate. Mosquito control is the primary defense against WNV + avian malaria.
Flock-level: drinking water vaccination. Individual valued birds: SC injection. Always record egg withdrawal time.
